1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Multiple Thai sources (rakbankerd farming guide, OPSMOAC Loei provincial extension report, Thai Forest Bulletin documenting wild cucumber in NW Thailand) confirm cucumber is grown across Thailand; UMN Extension anchors the temperature threshold for soil/seed establishment.

2_climate.rationale: UMN provides 70F soil-temperature threshold and standard climate guidance; rakbankerd anchors Thai short-day flowering biology; UF/IFAS Miami-Dade gives the closest tropical analogue to Thai conditions and an October-February main season.

3_soil.rationale: rakbankerd gives Thai pH 5.5-6.5 and 7-10 day field-prep practice; UMN gives international 6.0-6.5 range; OPSMOAC Loei adds field practice (mulch choice by season, Trichoderma seed treatment) tied to Thai soil reality.

4_water.rationale: UMN gives 1-inch/week water rule and drip-irrigation foliage-dry rationale; OPSMOAC Loei documents nightly 30-minute drip practice; KU root-rot research (Pythium aphanidermatum / Trichoderma T-50) documents flooding-related disease risk; rakbankerd gives furrow-irrigation and 2-3 day frequency.

5_planting.rationale: rakbankerd provides Thai direct-sow and short-day flowering practice; OPSMOAC Loei provides 25 cm spacing and seed Trichoderma coating; UMN provides international half-inch sowing depth and vining/bush spacing; Thaksin University hydroponic study supports the soilless/Japanese-cucumber section.

6_care.rationale: OPSMOAC Loei provides Thai farmer practice for fertilization timing and seasonal mulching; UF/IFAS provides fertigation framework; OPSMOAC Chiang Rai documents the air-circulation/weed-control rationale; Thaksin hydroponic study supports the fruit-shape early-wrap technique. Explicit deferral to กรมพัฒนาที่ดิน/กรมวิชาการเกษตร for site-specific rates.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: UC IPM provides comprehensive pest/disease taxonomy and Year-Round IPM 8-stage framework; OPSMOAC Chiang Rai anchors powdery-mildew Thai relevance; KU Trichoderma research anchors Pythium root-rot Thai relevance; UF/IFAS adds tropical-specific pests (melon thrips, melonworm, pickleworm); Genes 2021 review adds the 2004 downy-mildew resistance breakdown context. WarningBox in place; no specific chemical doses given.

8_harvest.rationale: rakbankerd gives 30-45 day harvest age; OPSMOAC Loei documents 30 harvest rounds vs 20-22 typical; UMN reinforces uniform-size pickling harvest and 'don't leave oversized fruit on vine' guidance.

9_economics.rationale: Market-context discussion is qualitative (no income/profit guarantees per SAFETY_POLICY); OPSMOAC Loei establishes commercial viability and harvest count, rakbankerd frames Thai market structure. Explicit refusal to forecast income reduces the rating to medium.

10_thailand_notes.rationale: Five Thai sources contribute to the regional and use-context breakdown: KU SSR aromatic-native-variety paper for genetic-conservation framing, Thai Forest Bulletin for NW-Thailand Karen-tribe wild form, OPSMOAC Loei for northeast practice, OPSMOAC Chiang Rai for north powdery-mildew warning, rakbankerd for general Thai practice.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: Four international sources directly support what foreign authorities say versus what does not transfer to Thailand; explicit Thailand-applicability framing for each (UMN temperate-zone caveat, UC IPM California-product caveat, UF/IFAS Florida varieties caveat, Genes 2021 genetic-resource framing).

overall_rationale: Twelve verified sources (eight Thai, four international) support every section, with multi-source coverage on every high-rated section. Section 9 (economics) downgraded to medium because Kaset Atlas declines income forecasts per SAFETY_POLICY.
